author: jordancoinbase_10730
content:
> One of the biggest blockers for sidecars which we have discussed with them so far is requiring proposers to sign the request to get the bid, as then the sidecar would need access to keys. I think we can make request signing optional and operators who want to use sidecars can choose not to send auth.

Notably, I don't think you need to give sidecar keys, it needs to have access to a signer.

IIUC the reason for this signing of the request is to validate they are indeed validator for the given block to keep the auction sealed bid? 

With the signed request, the auctioneer (proposer) could still leak the bid, but it's a trusted entity leaking. With a non signed the builder needs increased infrastructure/registration to attempt to seal bids (ie this proposer requests makes their requests from this IP usually, direct connect vpc). 

That could create some interesting dynamics auction theory wise (sealed v open). I think most people assume open bid is better for proposers but it might also increase potency of latency games, as its best not to reveal your best bid until late in the game?
